nodes.x: MY son, keep my words, and lay up my commandments with thee.
CHAPTER VIL : Solomon persuadeth to sincere wisdom— The cunning of a whore. : Y son, keep my words, and lay up my commandments with thee.
Keep my commandments, and live; and my law as the apple of thine eye.
Bind them upon thy fingers, write them upon the table of thine heart.
Say unto wisdom, Thou [art] my sister; and call understanding [thy] kinswoman:
with a double meaning: one the literal, the other figurative, as in the words “say to Wisdom, thou art my sister” (Prov. 7, 4). SAY NOW THOU ART MY SISTER.

AND THE MEN OF THE PLACE ASKED HIM OF HIS WIFE; AND HE SAID: SHE IS MY SISTER. Like Abraham before him, he referred with these words to the Shekinah, which was with him as well as with Rebekah his wife; for like Abraham he carried out the injunction: “Say unto wisdom: Thou art my sister” (Prov. 7, 4). They were further entitled to call her sister in virtue of the verse, “My sister, my love, my…
Abraham 2 25 Therefore say unto them, I pray thee, thou art my sister, that it may be well with me for thy sake, and my soul shall live because of thee.
Say, I pray, that thou art my sister, that it may be well with me for thy sake, and that my life may be spared on thy account.
That they may keep thee from the strange woman, from the stranger [which] flattereth with her words.
For at the window of my house I looked through my casement,
Window of my house Solomon, still speaking to his son hoping for him to identify with the "young man void of understanding." The "casement" was a lattice perhaps in the role of the old shutters.
And beheld among the simple ones, I discerned among the youths, a young man void of understanding,
Passing through the street near her corner; and he went the way to her house,
In the twilight, in the evening, in the black and dark night:
Evening, in the black and dark of night Literally, "in the pupil [of the eye] of the night."

I called on Sister Robinson in the twilight of evening, and
asked for the dinner which I had spoken for one and a half years
before. We were kindly received by her and others.
And, behold, there met him a woman [with] the attire of an harlot, and subtil of heart.
With the attire of an harlot At this point he knew where his feet were going. This was his moment of escape
(She [is] loud and stubborn; her feet abide not in her house
Now [is she] without, now in the streets, and lieth in wait at every corner.)
28 "Mockery and reproach (are the lot) of the proud, _ And vengeance, like a lion, lieth in wait for them'>.
So she caught him, and kissed him, [and] with an impudent face said unto him,
[I have] peace offerings with me; this day have I payed my vows.
8 whole burnt-offerings, first-fruits, freewill-offerings, peace-offerings. And each day he was instructing
Then followed the special sacrificial offerings for the day and the chanting, to instrumental
Therefore came I forth to meet thee, diligently to seek thy face, and I have found thee.
I have decked my bed with coverings of tapestry, with carved [works], with fine linen of Egypt.
I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.

IS IT A SMALL MATTER THAT THOU HAST TAKEN AWAY MY HUSBAND? The intent thereof is as follows: “Is it a small matter that you take my husband unto yourself as if you are his wife and I am the handmaid? Will you now also make yourself the mistress to take the mandrakes in whose odor I delight?”It is possible that Rachel wanted the mandrakes in honor of Jacob to perfume his couch. Leah had done in…
Come, let us take our fill of love until the morning: let us solace ourselves with loves.
For the goodman [is] not at home, he is gone a long journey:

The executioner must have gone a long journey, and which could scarcely be performed in two days from Tiberias, where the tyrant's court was, to execute that bloody command. So that that horrid dish, the head of the venerable prophet, could not be presented to the maid but some days after the celebration of his birthday.
He hath taken a bag of money with him, [and] will come home at the day appointed.
With her much fair speech she caused him to yield, with the flattering of her lips she forced him.
He goeth after her straightway, as an ox goeth to the slaughter, or as a fool to the correction of the stocks;
Till a dart strike through his liver; as a bird hasteth to the snare, and knoweth not that it [is] for his life.
Hearken unto me now therefore, O ye children, and attend to the words of my mouth.
Let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths.
For she hath cast down many wounded: yea, many strong [men] have been slain by her.
Her house [is] the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death.
